preparation_method: topped with tomato sauce and browned butter
main_ingredients: spices (cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, chili, garam masala)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Chicken is approximately 400 g, comprising sliced tandoori chicken over pide bread, topped with tomato sauce, browned butter, and a side of plain yogurt.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, tandoori, cooked; composite dish estimation based on USDA FoodData Central entries for tandoori chicken (FDC ID 2097663) and Turkish pide bread, with tomato sauce and butter components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Chicken as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for tandoori-style cooked chicken (~55% of dish weight), Turkish pide/flatbread (~20%), tomato sauce (~15%), and browned butter (~10%), weighted by approximate serving proportions. Values represent per-100 g of the composite plated dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; pita/flatbread GI reference (GI ~57) used as analogous estimate for pide bread component; mixed dish GI estimated by carbohydrate-weighted method
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Chicken. GI estimated at 55 (low) using carbohydrate-weighted method: pide flatbread GI ~57 (University of Sydney GI Database, white pita bread analogue), protein and fat components suppress overall GI. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (55 × 13.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 4) / 100 = 30.4 g. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per 400 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single 400 g serving provides approximately 1680 mg sodium, representing 73% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size, request reduced-salt preparation, and avoid adding extra salt or high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~55) driven by pide flatbread; moderate carbohydrate load per serving (~55 g per 400 g plate)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The pide bread base contributes a moderate glycemic load (~30 GL per serving); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider reducing the bread portion, pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables, and monitoring postprandial glucose response, as the high protein and fat content partially attenuates the glycemic rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from browned butter (~3.8 g per 100 g; ~15 g per serving) and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The browned butter topping contributes significant saturated fat (~15 g per 400 g serving), which exceeds the AHA recommended daily limit of 13 g saturated fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should request reduced butter, choose lean chicken breast over thigh, and limit serving frequency to occasional consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Chicken is a fusion variant of the classic Turkish Iskender Kebab (İskender Kebap), originating from Bursa, Turkey, where it was created by İskender Efendi in the 19th century. The traditional dish features thinly sliced döner lamb or beef layered over torn pieces of pide flatbread, drenched in a rich tomato sauce and finished tableside with sizzling browned butter, accompanied by plain yogurt. In this Singapore restaurant adaptation, the protein is replaced with tandoori-marinated chicken — chicken marinated in yogurt and aromatic spices including cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, and garam masala, then roasted at high heat in a tandoor oven or equivalent — producing a smoky, charred exterior with a moist, spiced interior. The dish is served as a substantial plated meal: the pide bread absorbs the tomato sauce and butter, creating a soft, flavour-saturated base, while the tandoori chicken provides a contrasting smoky, slightly charred top layer. The yogurt on the side provides a cooling, acidic counterpoint. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, high-protein meal (~195 kcal per 100 g, ~14.5 g protein per 100 g) with notable saturated fat from the butter component and moderate sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), driven primarily by the pide bread. Clinically, the dish is of moderate concern for hypertension due to sodium, for Type 2 Diabetes due to the moderate glycemic load from the bread base, and for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat from browned butter. It is halal-suitable when prepared with halal-certified chicken and contains gluten and dairy allergens.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Iskender Kebab with Tandoori Chicken as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for tandoori chicken, flatbread, tomato sauce, and butter, weighted by approximate serving proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on pide flatbread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite dish estimation; vitamin D data is absent from the primary source records for tandoori chicken, pide bread, tomato sauce, and butter components consulted, and therefore cannot be derived for the composite. The dish name in the input ('74. Iskender Kebab (Tandoori Chicken)') includes a menu numbering prefix ('74.') which has been removed from the canonical food_name. GI is estimated at 55 (low) using carbohydrate-weighted method with pide flatbread as the primary glycemic driver; direct GI measurement is recommended for higher confidence. The dish is served at Sultan Palace Turkish and Lebanese Cuisine, Boat Quay, Singapore, via field survey at SGD 31.90. Halal status is flagged as likely true given the Turkish-Lebanese restaurant context in Singapore, but formal halal certification has not been independently verified. No zero-value core macros are present in this record; the zero-macro justification rule does not apply.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with component-weighted nutrient estimation from USDA FoodData Central. Added multilingual names for Chinese (simplified/traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi to satisfy G6 for Turkish dish served in Singapore. Added romanized Turkish local name. Documented all -1 sentinel values. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Set GI to 55 with analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database pide flatbread data.
Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to align with glycemic_index value of 55. Strengthened notes field regarding vitamin_d_mcg sentinel value.

Iteration 3: Fixed g3_numeric_hygiene failure by explicitly documenting in the notes field that vitamin_d_mcg is -1 because vitamin D data is absent from the USDA FoodData Central source records for all component entries (tandoori chicken, pide bread, tomato sauce, butter) used in the composite estimation, satisfying the sentinel values documentation rule.
